questions.question: What type of frame does an ARP request use?
questions.explanation: ARP requests are sent as Layer 2 broadcasts (destination ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff) so all hosts on the segment receive them.

questions.question: What does ARP resolve?
questions.explanation: ARP resolves a known IPv4 address to an unknown MAC address so the host can build a Layer 2 frame.

questions.question: What is a gratuitous ARP?
options: An ARP request for another host's MAC address
An ARP reply sent without a corresponding request
An ARP request sent by a router on behalf of a host
An ARP reply that contains an incorrect MAC address
questions.explanation: A gratuitous ARP is an unsolicited ARP reply a host sends to announce or update its own IP-to-MAC mapping.

questions.question: Which ICMPv6 message type is used for IPv6 address resolution (similar to ARP request)?
questions.explanation: Neighbor Solicitation (NS, Type 135) is the IPv6 equivalent of an ARP request, asking a target to respond with its MAC address.

questions.question: What destination address does an NDP Neighbor Solicitation use instead of broadcast?
questions.explanation: NS messages are sent to the solicited-node multicast address, which is derived from the last 24 bits of the target IPv6 address. This is more efficient than broadcast.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Duplicate Address Detection (DAD)?
options: To detect duplicate MAC addresses on the network
To verify an IPv6 address is unique before using it
To find duplicate ARP entries in the cache
questions.explanation: DAD sends a Neighbor Solicitation for the host's own address. If no one responds, the address is unique and safe to use.

questions.question: Which Cisco IOS command displays the ARP table on a router?
questions.explanation: The 'show arp' command displays the ARP cache on a Cisco router, showing IP-to-MAC address mappings.

questions.question: What security feature mitigates ARP spoofing attacks?
questions.explanation: Dynamic ARP Inspection (DAI) validates ARP packets against the DHCP snooping binding table to prevent ARP spoofing attacks.

questions.question: What is Proxy ARP?
options: A host responding to ARP requests for its own address
A router answering ARP requests on behalf of hosts on another network
A switch flooding ARP requests to all ports
A firewall filtering malicious ARP packets
questions.explanation: Proxy ARP allows a router to respond to ARP requests for hosts on a different subnet, using its own MAC address so it can forward the traffic.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of NDP over ARP?
options: NDP uses broadcasts for better compatibility
NDP operates at Layer 2 for faster processing
NDP uses multicast instead of broadcast, reducing unnecessary traffic
NDP does not require any security mechanisms
questions.explanation: NDP uses solicited-node multicast addresses instead of broadcasts, meaning only relevant hosts process the message. This is more efficient than ARP's broadcast approach.
